close_reason: Style: turbofish in async block is necessary for type inference
description: src/lib.rs:292 uses Ok::<_, SecretError>((client, org_id, project_id, secret_id)) inside an async block. The turbofish is required because the async block's return type can't be inferred from the inner expression alone (the closure passed to get_or_try_init is generic and the error type only flows from the .await? operators above it). The alternative is to annotate the closure return type or to bind the result with a let-Ok pattern. Both are clunkier than the turbofish. Filed as opinion because the turbofish is the cleanest existing fix; documenting it here so a future reviewer doesn't 'fix' it.
title: Ok::<_, SecretError>(...) turbofish in async block
